title: Quality Audit — Silent Catches, TODOs, Error Handling, Duplicated Patterns
summary: 15 findings across silent error swallowing, inconsistent error types, console.* usage bypassing the structured logger, and one TODO with a concrete action plan. ErrorBoundary wrapping from round 3 is correct.

errorBoundaryVerification.details: ErrorBoundary from src/ui/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx correctly wraps: (1) entire app in AppProviders.tsx, (2) desktop sidebar in AppLayout.tsx with fallback text, (3) main view area with key-based reset, (4) mobile drawer sidebar with fallback, (5) detail panel with fallback, (6) AI chat in AIChat.tsx with fallback, (7) plugin views via PluginErrorBoundary in PluginView.tsx. All boundaries log via createLogger and provide user-visible reset buttons.

findings.description: Two .catch(() => setTTSVoices([])) and .catch(() => setTTSModels([])) silently swallow errors when fetching TTS voices/models. A console.warn or log.warn would help diagnose provider configuration issues.
findings.suggestion: Add log.warn('[voice] Failed to fetch TTS voices/models:', err) before setting empty fallback.

findings.description: Three .catch(() => ...) calls silently swallow errors when loading storage info, task count, and plugin count for the About tab. Failures here are invisible to developers.
findings.suggestion: Add console.warn with context prefix for each catch.

findings.description: .catch(() => setSavedFilter(null)) silently swallows errors when loading saved filters. Filter loading failures become invisible.
findings.suggestion: Log the error before falling back to null.

findings.description: .catch(() => { ... }) silently handles model fetch failures. The catch block likely sets an empty state without logging.
findings.suggestion: Add log.warn for debugging AI provider configuration.

findings.description: .catch(() => { ... }) silently swallows microphone permission or enumeration errors.
findings.suggestion: Log the error — microphone failures are common and hard to diagnose without logs.

findings.description: reader.cancel().catch(() => {}) completely swallows stream cancellation errors. While cancellation errors are often benign, a debug-level log would aid troubleshooting.
findings.suggestion: Acceptable as-is — stream cancel errors are expected. Optionally add log.debug.

findings.description: throw new Error('Cannot create relation: would create a cycle') uses generic Error instead of ValidationError from src/core/errors.ts. This is the only throw in core/tasks.ts that doesn't use the custom error types.
findings.suggestion: Change to throw new ValidationError('Cannot create relation: would create a cycle').

findings.description: Three throw new Error() calls in plugin loader use generic Error. 'Plugin not found' should be NotFoundError; 'no default export' and 'cannot remove built-in' should be ValidationError for consistent catch handling.
findings.suggestion: Import and use NotFoundError/ValidationError from core/errors.ts.

findings.description: Uses console.warn directly instead of the structured createLogger utility. The rest of the codebase has a mix — some UI files use createLogger, others use console.* directly.
findings.suggestion: Adopt createLogger consistently in UI code, or at minimum use it in non-trivial modules.

findings.description: Uses console.error directly for filter persistence failures. Should use createLogger for structured logging consistency.
findings.suggestion: Replace with const log = createLogger('filters'); log.error(...).

findings.description: Uses console.warn and console.error directly for settings load/persist failures. Other context files (VoiceContext) use createLogger.

TODO: PERF-301 — Replace 52 individual api.getAppSetting() calls with a single batch endpoint. This is a legitimate performance issue — 52 HTTP requests on every settings mount. The TODO includes a clear implementation plan.
findings.suggestion: Track as a backlog item. Implement GET /api/settings batch endpoint.

findings.description: Four 'as any' casts in piper-local-tts.ts. These interact with external WASM libraries and browser APIs (OPFS directory iteration) that lack proper TypeScript types. Acceptable given the external API constraints.
findings.suggestion: Consider adding type declarations for piper-tts-web if the library matures.

findings.description: Two 'as any' casts for SpeechRecognition / webkitSpeechRecognition browser API. Standard pattern for accessing vendor-prefixed Web Speech API.
findings.suggestion: Acceptable — could add a SpeechRecognition type declaration file but low priority.

findings.description: ~15 UI files use raw console.warn/console.error while ~7 files use createLogger. The codebase has a structured logger (src/utils/logger.ts) but UI code inconsistently adopts it. This makes it harder to control log levels or add log aggregation later.
findings.suggestion: Standardize: either adopt createLogger in all UI code or document that console.* is acceptable in UI catch blocks for non-critical errors.
